IRS990.Desc: SINCE 1971, CRI-HELP HAS BEEN A LEADER IN EFFECTIVE DRUG AND ALCOHOL TREATMENT IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. OUR MISSION IS FULFILLED THROUGH INTEGRATED PROGRAMS, INCLUDING DETOXIFICATION, RESIDENTIAL REHABILITATION, AFTERCARE, AND DRUG-FREE LIVING, OFFERED IN ENGLISH AND SPANISH. SERVICES ARE DELIVERED IN SAFE, SUPPORTIVE ENVIRONMENTS AND ENHANCED BY GRIEF COUNSELING, EDUCATIONAL AND VOCATIONAL SUPPORT, AND 12-STEP PROGRAM PARTICIPATION. ALL STAFF ARE CERTIFIED IN SUBSTANCE COUNSELING UNDER LICENSED CLINICIANS' SUPERVISION. WE ALSO MAINTAIN ONE OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA'S MOST DYNAMIC ALUMNI PROGRAMS, WITH OVER 400 VOLUNTEERS AND ALUMNI AIDING CLIENT TRANSITIONS TO COMMUNITY LIVING.

BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: DR DAN BEGAL DBA ACOUSTIC PSYCHOMETRY SY

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: MANAGEMENT FEES AND OTHER INCOME - 2019 AMOUNT: $ 91,552. 2020 AMOUNT: $ 145,319. 2021 AMOUNT: $ 445,089. 2022 AMOUNT: $ 113,153. 2023 AMOUNT: $ 38,978.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: GOVERNMENT GRANT REPORTED ON FORM 990, PART VIII AND SCHEDULE A, PART II
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION RECEIVED A GOVERNMENT GRANT FROM: COUNTY OF LA - DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES (DHSP) COUNTY OF LA - DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES (SAPC) ADVOCATES FOR HUMAN POTENTIAL, INC. (AHP) MEDICATION ASSISTED TREATMENT (MAT) GRANT PROGRAM DURING THE TAX YEAR. THIS GRANT WAS USED TO SUPPORT SUBSTANCE ABUSE. FOR PURPOSES OF FORM 990, PART VIII, THE GRANT IS REPORTED AS PROGRAM SERVICE REVENUE ON LINE 1E, AS IT REPRESENTS PAYMENT FOR SPECIFIC SERVICES RENDERED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE ORGANIZATION'S EXEMPT PURPOSE. FOR PURPOSES OF SCHEDULE A, PART II, THE GRANT IS APPROPRIATELY REPORTED AS A GOVERNMENT GRANT ON LINES 1 AND 5, AS THE SOURCE OF THE FUNDING IS A GOVERNMENT ENTITY AND QUALIFIES AS PUBLIC SUPPORT UNDER THE IRS PUBLIC CHARITY TEST. THIS DUAL TREATMENT IS CONSISTENT WITH IRS GUIDELINES, WHICH DISTINGUISH BETWEEN THE NATURE OF INCOME (PART VIII) AND THE SOURCE OF SUPPORT (SCHEDULE A).

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CRI-HELP, INC. IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND IS EXEMPT FROM CALIFORNIA STATE FRANCHISE TAXES UNDER SECTION 23701(D) OF THE CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE ORGANIZATION HAS UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME, AS DEFINED BY SECTION 509(A)(1) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE, THAT INCOME MAY BE SUBJECT TO FEDERAL INCOME TAX. HOWEVER, THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT DURING THE YEAR ENDED JUNE 30, 2024, IT HAD ANY NET UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME AND ACCORDINGLY, NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES HAS BEEN RECORDED IN THE ACCOMPANYING CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. THE ORGANIZATION FOLLOWS THE GUIDANCE OF FASB ASC 740, ACCOUNTING FOR INCOME TAXES, RELATED TO UNCERTAINTIES IN INCOME TAXES, WHICH PRESCRIBES A THRESHOLD OF MORE LIKELY THAN NOT FOR RECOGNITION AND DERECOGNITION OF TAX POSITIONS TAKEN OR EXPECTED TO BE TAKEN IN A TAX RETURN. THE ORGANIZATION HAS DETERMINED THAT THERE ARE NO MATERIAL UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT REQUIRE RECOGNITION OR DISCLOSURE IN THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

CRI-HELP, INC, ENGAGES IN GAMING ACTIVITIES, NAMELY BINGO AND INSTANT BINGO/PULL-TABS. THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT REPORT THE REVENUE FROM ITS BINGO GAMING ACTIVITIES AS UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME BECAUSE THE GAMES MEET THE DEFINITION OF BINGO UNDER THE CODE & REGULATIONS, DO NOT VIOLATE STATE OR LOCAL LAW, AND ARE PLAYED IN A JURISDICTION WHERE BINGO GAMES ARE NOT REGULARLY CARRIED ON BY FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS. FURTHERMORE, THE ORGANIZATION OPERATES ITS GAMING USING VOLUNTEERS, WITH SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF THE WORK OF THE ORGANIZATION'S GAMING OPERATIONS PERFORMED BY VOLUNTEERS (INDIVIDUALS WHO ARE NOT COMPENSATED). ACCORDINGLY, THE ORGANIZATION MEETS THE EXCEPTION FOR REPORTING ANY GAMING REVENUE (BINGO, INSTANT BINGO OR PULL-TABS) AS UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME AND THEREFORE, HAS NO REQUIREMENT TO FILE IRS FORM 990T [TREASURY REGULATION SECTION 1.6012-2(E)] SINCE THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT HAVE UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME.

COMPENSATION OF EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR: THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S SALARY IS REVIEWED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD USING THE ASSOCIATION COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS STUDY AS WELL AS THE CHARITY NAVIGATOR CEO COMPENSATION STUDY.
JACK BERNSTEIN RECEIVED NONFIXED PAYMENTS. THESE PAYMENTS WERE DETERMINED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S PRESIDENT/CEO AND CHAIRMAN OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS WILL CAREFULLY REVIEW THE FORM 990 PRIOR TO SIGNING AND MAILING THE RETURN TO THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EACH BOARD MEMBER SIGNS A CONFLICT OF INTEREST ANNUALLY AND IF ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST ARISE DURING THE YEAR, THEY ARE REQUIRED TO DISCLOSE THEM. NEW MEMBERS SIGN THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY UPON ELECTION TO THE BOARD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S SALARY IS REVIEWED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD USING THE ASSOCIATION COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS STUDY AS WELL AS THE CHARITY NAVIGATOR CEO COMPENSATION STUDY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, AND OTHER PERTINENT DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
THE PROCESS HAS NOT CHANGED FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.
